scenario_name: Email triage assistant with attachment OCR
description: An organization deploys an AI assistant that reads each user's inbox, summarizes messages, drafts replies, and extracts action items. Attachments are OCR'd to bring image-based content into the context window. The assistant is a privileged endpoint integration with access to every email in the user's mailbox — DSGAI16 endpoint overreach plus DSGAI09 multimodal capture combine to make this archetype unusually exposed.
assumptions: Assistant runs server-side against the user's mailbox under delegated OAuth.
Inbox includes mail from external senders; attachments routinely contain PII.
Hosted LLM under enterprise data-isolation contract.

components.description: Attachment OCR service; processes images, PDFs, scanned documents.
Injection-classifier on email body and OCR output; PII redaction on response display.
Audit log of summaries and drafted replies; field-redacted.

risks_identified.rationale: Inbox content + attachments contain PII, financial data, contracts, credentials shared in error, customer correspondence.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Output PII redaction at display; semantic content still in the LLM context.

risks_identified.rationale: Every email from an external sender is potential indirect prompt injection; any prompt-injection-bearing email reaches the assistant whether the user opens it or not.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Injection-classifier on email body and OCR output; no auto-tool-action without user confirmation. EchoLeak demonstrated the residual risk class.

risks_identified.rationale: Attachment OCR pulls image text into the model context; visual prompt injection in attachments is in scope.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Injection-classifier on OCR output; flagged attachments held for human review.

risks_identified.rationale: Mailbox-wide context for summarization is structurally over-broad.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Per-summary chunking helps; semantic relevance gate not deployed.

risks_identified.rationale: Endpoint-class reach: the assistant sees everything the user can see, including private correspondence.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: User-consent obtained; per-folder opt-out exists; reach remains inherent.

risks_identified.rationale: GDPR Art. 5 (proportionality), CCPA, HIPAA (some inboxes), professional-privilege concerns.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: DPIA on file; HIPAA-scoped inboxes excluded by configuration.

risks_identified.rationale: Audit logs of summaries can contain extracted PII at scale.
Phishing campaigns target the assistant — adversarial emails designed to manipulate AI-drafted replies into endorsing fraudulent requests.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Drafted replies require user review; trusted-output-components hardening (no auto-link inclusion).

existing_controls.description: Injection-classifier on email body and OCR output.
No autonomous tool action; user reviews every drafted reply.
Output PII redaction at display; audit-log redaction at emission.
Per-folder opt-out for sensitive correspondence folders.
Drafted replies do not auto-include links from the source email.

control_gaps.description: Mailbox-wide summarization is structurally over-broad; no semantic relevance gate.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Limit summarization to user-selected threads; require explicit selection for mailbox-wide views.

control_gaps.description: Indirect-injection-via-email class is documented in production assistants (EchoLeak); residual risk acceptance is not formalized.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Document leadership residual-risk acceptance; deploy EchoLeak-style defenses (no markdown rendering to arbitrary external hosts; strict CSP).

control_gaps.description: Attachment-OCR pipeline scans for OCR injection but does not flag low-contrast or hidden-text patterns in PDFs.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Add visual-anomaly detection on attachments; flag for human review.

framework_alignments.control_name: Approaches for mapping legal and AI risks
